title: Asukas Gaijin Identity
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's Gaijin Identity             Quote Post by Maru » Tue Aug 28, 2012 12:26 am In a discussion in the Fandom area on good Asuka cosplay, this nugget of insight appeared: arkiel wrote: I always preferred Asuka have caucasian features, but all evidence points to me putting more thought into Asuka's gaijin identity than everyone that ever worked on any of the Evangelion projects, put together. ^several people on this board also meet this distinction.

[]: Re: Asuka's Gaijin Identity             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Tue Aug 28, 2012 12:46 pm Asuka is part-Japanese. I think Asuka isn't the type to be worried at being treated like an outsider; she already sees herself as special because she pilots Eva. Hikari attempts to reach out to her as Asuka goes on her downward spiral and she barely notices. Whether the other kids might be treating her well or poorly, she was already lost in her own mind too much.

[]: Re: Asuka's Gaijin Identity             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Tue Aug 28, 2012 1:21 pm JoeD80 wrote: Asuka is part-Japanese. I think Asuka isn't the type to be worried at being treated like an outsider; she already sees herself as special because she pilots Eva. Hikari attempts to reach out to her as Asuka goes on her downward spiral and she barely notices. Whether the other kids might be treating her well or poorly, she was already lost in her own mind too much.

[]: Re: Asuka's Gaijin Identity             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Aug 28, 2012 1:29 pm JoeD80 wrote: Asuka is part-Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Aug 28, 2012 3:41 pm The thing is, she has some knowdledge of the customs (after all, she was shown to have known her Japanese grandmother and speaks the language) and being an EVa pilot immediately gave her instant star-status on the school , which would have overridden any rejection based on her hair color/outsider-ness. When she came to that school, she had already participated in saving all their butts, ie, done something for them to judge her based on her own actions rather than their conceptions about a big group of people, they would think of her as "the eva pilot girl" before they'd think of her as "the foreign chick". I mean, they didn't bully a girl with over-average intelligence and lack of social skills, (much more so than your average person without social skills), maybe the ppl at that school are fairly decent people? (Okay, they whispered about her being retarded and no one befriended her, but we can chalk that up to Rei's own limitations and human beings being imperfect. In many places, a person like her would've had the living daylights bullied out of her. ) As we see it, she immidiately surrounded herself with a flock of friends - of course, the only real friends she had were Shinji and Hikari(sort of, she kept even them at distance), everyonbe else just saw her pretty facade, but that's not because they didn't accept her, but because Asuka didn't accept herself and thus made her facade, no appearance-based bullying involved. And as for the Rei thing, there's no way they'd have her use an edgy, unusual-for-such-situations word like "convenient" if it wasn't for a reason. At best, she wants everybody to like her, and took the fact that someone didn't love her on first sight as a personal insult. In that case, that would make her a person with an overblown sense of entitlement. But most likely, all Asuka thought was really just "What a weirdo." I mean, just look at the scene: Before she starts speaking, Asuka positions herself above Rei and penetrantly blocks the sun, she doesn't walk up to Rei and catch her attebntion, no, she insists that Rei has to be the one to notice her. and she immediately calls her "Pilot of the prototype ". From the beginning, Asuka was convinced that she is better than Rei and intends to make her feel it. No ideal prerequisite for friendship if you ask me. She already had her opinion of Rei finished and packaded. Don't you normally become friends after you get to know someone and decide you like them? (Rei probably did not decline because of any of that, I think she just did not get that most people equal "I do not exactly like you" with "I dislike you". Her opinion of some person she just met is "neutral" at first. She says "I'll do it if I'm ordered to" because Rei, at this point, would do anything of she's ordered to, but it means that she currently doesn't see any reason/incentive of her own. She only just met Asuka. ) It is true that Asuka and Rei have some things in common, but Asuka does not see that al all. You don't have to fear something you understand, something that's just like you. there are multiple points where Asuka accuses Rei of having several bad traits she herself has, without grasping any similarity. Asuka has it in her head that she must be different from everyone else. She does not look for similarities, they are things she doesn't want to see. She doesn't want to see way more obvious similarities in Misato and Shinji, either. The "what kind of person is she..." comes long after Asuka declared it a war, it's more that she realized that Rei is NOT just very arrogant as she first thought, and based on the elevator scene, the conclusion she arrived on was "Rei is the emotionless spawn of the uncanny valley" She is not worrying about Rei's motivations/trying to understand her, she just made the legitimate observation that no one knows much about her. Sadness and curiosity? Not really. Not at all. All because Rei offered to take a mission Asuka herself refused. Asuka talks to Shinji about beating and defeating her/taking revenge, in episode 10, she's already teasing her ("No hot springs for you!") and violently grabs her arm at some point, and episode 11... episode 11... that was downright cruel, talking badly about her all the time and never ceasing to insult her. And despite all this, the only time Rei returned any animosity was when Asuka insulted Shinji who was currently MIA/presumed dead. I think we can all agree with Rei that this was going to far, no matter how understandable Asuka's motivations are. I give you that the ramen stand bit was a genuine act of empathy on Asuka's side that was showing that she was beginning to improve/become better person/seriously care about her flatmates before later occurences sent her down the spiral, but her "need" for a big juicy piece of meat isn't that big of a need to forego, it's more like she's "not being mean" - earlier, she made fun of Shinji for trying to take Misato's feelings into account and insisted on going to a really expenside restaurant. She was genuinely trying to be nice, but to a "She's a human being, not a complete monster" extent, not to a" She's such a compassionate, golden hearted person!" extents, most of the time, Asuka displays a disturbing lack of empathy. It's like she doesn't know that Shinji, Misato and Rei have feelings. She constantly insults them without thinking about what they must be feeling, she weants to be kissed, but she won't give Shinji the satisfaction of being wanted by her. She won't even let him breathe, because SHE finds it uncomfortable. She trashtalks Shinji when he's in such nice situations as handcuffed in a cell or missing in action because a big black blob ate him alive. That she has a reason for being the way she is/isn't pure evil doesn't change that her actions DISREGARD and, at times, really HURT other people. In Rebuild, she's even surprised that the person she percieves as a romantic rival is... *gasp* genuinely in love with the guy! and finding similarities between her and Rei comes as a big schock. Of course, it's not the same continiuty, but it's made by the same people with the original in the back of their minds. They were assuming that having a redhaired girl named Asuka make these realizations would come as a shocking twist to us. Sorry, you can tell me that Asuka is just a sad misguided girl and that she can't be blamed for her actions, but that she just wants to be Rei's friend? I might have bought it if you'd said "Shinji" or "Misato", but Rei? No. That wasn't what we saw in this show at all. Hyper Shinchan wrote: ,she only seems to have issues with kanji from what we can see in the series.

[]: Re: Asuka's Gaijin Identity             Quote Post by Maru » Tue Aug 28, 2012 4:39 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Yeah, she's an hafu ("half"), it seems to make her situation at least partially different from a gaijin proper. Does it really get lost that often? I have the impression that I read similar phrases in several threads about Asuka... She speaks it fluently enough to use it as primary language in the interface with the Eva, she only seems to have issues with kanji from what we can see in the series. I don't think that she was bothered either positively or negatively by her rejection, anyone would have just thought "this girl sure is weird" and in fact this is exactly her reaction. also Asuka was probably aware that she was being "a little" imposing and she could have expected even a worse reaction, people normally don't directly ask you to become friends out of convenience and I doubt that she just picked the wrong words, the whole scene with Asuka standing out from above seems to give a very different impression.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Aug 28, 2012 6:00 pm Kendrix wrote: episode 11... episode 11... that was downright cruel, talking badly about her all the time and never ceasing to insult her.

[]:             Quote Post by Maru » Tue Aug 28, 2012 9:28 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I was referring mainly to the Japanese reaction to foreigners, they actually do call her "gaijin" now that I'm watching episode 9 again but she essentially becomes the idol of the school, it doesn't look like they give her the cold shoulder. Overall she's integrated fairly well in both school and work, Asuka's problems seem to lie more in her personality than her current environment, I don't think that she had any real friend or bond in Germany either, she always gave me the impression that other people exist mainly to serve her needs, like Kendrix said she seems to have little to no consideration for other people's feelings.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Aug 29, 2012 12:12 pm If they ever remake the TV series, the gaijin theme could nicely flesh out the tepid middle episodes. I toyed around with writing a oneshot that starts on Over the Rainbow and ends around episode 23/24 (wherever the bathroom scene is at), where Asuka realizes with disgust that she's started to -think- in Japanese. But my culture shock experience is from wandering around Kyoto and Tokyo on my own for a week (I slept in a train station overnight, yay!). I don't have a good sense of the deep-down crazies that keep you buried and shivering under a stack of futons because you think the Japanese language is trying to kill you. Someone more experienced should probably turn their hand to it, though. I think its mostly down with discovering how much of yourself exists with reference to your environment, and learning to compensate when those external references are gone and replaced with something weird and different (and probably de facto insane). If you happen to be unstable and naive and arrogant (like Asuka), I assume it becomes more and more of a challenge to maintain your own illusions whilst rebuilding them and other fundamental parts of yourself with reference to an external environment you don't totally understand. There are also other fun things you could do with the gaijin identity. The world of Evangelion is one where the Japanese have been nuked three times, now. I don't think the identity of the nuking nation is known in canon, but consider the assimilation/reaction of the Japanese following the first two nukes, and maybe Asuka's status as an Eva pilot isn't the only reason why she's a rock star.
